page.content: The first table presents correlation coefficient values across variables related to diabetes, specifically focusing on Nondiabetic, Diabetes without retinopathy, and Diabetic retinopathy. For age, the correlation coefficients are −0.181 for Nondiabetic, −0.015 for Diabetes without retinopathy, and 0.047 for Diabetic retinopathy. Glucose shows a correlation of 0.030 for Nondiabetic, −0.021 for Diabetes without retinopathy, and 0.001 for Diabetic retinopathy. The duration variable is not applicable for Nondiabetic with a correlation of −0.049 for Diabetes without retinopathy and 0.016 for Diabetic retinopathy. Lastly, HbA1C displays a correlation of 0.092 for Nondiabetic, −0.177 for Diabetes without retinopathy, and 0.051 for Diabetic retinopathy. The second table delineates the incidence and characteristics of two bacterial strains, AE-VTEC O157:H7 and Agg-VTEC O104:H4. The incidence of sporadic cases for AE-VTEC O157:H7 is described as the third bacterial cause of diarrhoea in developed countries, while Agg-VTEC O104:H4 is extremely rarely reported. Outbreaks of AE-VTEC O157:H7 are frequent, ranging from a few to over 6000 cases, contrasted by one outbreak of Agg-VTEC O104:H4 with almost 4000 cases.

The second table delineates the incidence and characteristics of two bacterial strains, AE-VTEC O157:H7 and Agg-VTEC
O104:H4. The incidence of sporadic cases for AE-VTEC O157:H7 is described as the third bacterial cause of diarrhoea in

page.html:
AE-VTEC O157:H7Agg-VTEC O104:H4
Incidence of sporadic casesThird bacterial cause of diarrhoea in developed countriesExtremely rarely reported
OutbreaksFrequent (from a few to > 6000 cases)One outbreak (almost 4000 cases)
Age distributionYoung children (elderly)Middle aged adults
Gender distributionEqualPredominance of female
Incubation1 to 9 days (average 3-5 days)8 days (interquartile range 7 to 9 days)
Progression to HUS7-10%> 20%
Mortality0.5%1.4%

cells.cell_text: Third bacterial cause of diarrhoea in developed countries

page.content: Age distribution for AE-VTEC O157:H7 includes young children and the elderly, while Agg-VTEC O104:H4 affects middle-aged adults. Gender distribution is equal for AE-VTEC O157:H7 but shows a predominance of females for Agg-VTEC O104:H4. Incubation periods range from 1 to 9 days (average 3-5 days) for AE-VTEC O157:H7, whereas Agg-VTEC O104:H4 has a duration of 8 days (interquartile range 7 to 9 days). The progression to HUS is 7-10% for AE-VTEC O157:H7 and exceeds 20% for Agg-VTEC O104:H4, with mortality rates at 0.5% and 1.4%, respectively.
